hero.headline: Understand what the market is doing, right now.
hero.subtitle: Stock Sense reads trend, momentum, and structure, then explains the current market state in plain English.
hero.clarity: It does not predict the future. It interprets the present.

howItWorks.title: Three steps. No chart expertise needed.
howItWorks.subtitle: If you can type a ticker, you can use this.

1.title: Enter a ticker
1.detail: Type AAPL, TSLA, BTC, or any symbol you want to understand.

2.title: Click Analyze
2.detail: We read the latest price behavior and build a market interpretation.

3.title: Read the plain-English output
3.detail: See what the market state means, what to watch, and where risk is higher.

liveExample.title: This is what your output looks like.
liveExample.subtitle: A real, plain-English interpretation card - not a prediction feed.
liveExample.explanation: Buyers still have control, but the pace is cooling. Short-term pullback risk is rising.
liveExample.explanationSimple: Price is still mostly going up, but not as strongly. A short dip can happen.

trustLayer.title: This is not financial advice. This is an interpretation engine.
trustLayer.body: We do not predict future prices. We explain market behavior using trend, momentum, and structure so beginners can make calmer decisions.
trustLayer.disclaimer: Use this as context, then apply your own plan and risk rules.

analyze.subtitleStocks: Start with any US-listed stock and get a plain-English market read.
analyze.subtitleCrypto: Start with any crypto symbol and get a plain-English market read.

errors.cryptoFormat: Crypto: 2-12 chars, A-Z and 0-9, must start with a letter (e.g. BTC, PEPE).
errors.loadFailed: Load failed. Check your connection and try again.

trust.primary: This is an interpretation of recent price behavior, not a forecast. Past patterns do not guarantee future results.
trust.secondary: Validation: methodology review in progress - treat outputs as educational context only.

chart.title: Price context
chart.subtitleStocks: Daily closes: the window the interpretation engine reads from.
chart.subtitleCrypto: Daily closes sampled from a 24/7 tape - each bar is one close, not the full path.
chart.cryptoNote: Crypto realizes wider ranges than equities; read dispersion as well as slope.
chart.captionBeginner: The line shows whether price is moving in a clear direction or drifting sideways.
chart.captionAdvanced: Price with moving averages (20 / 50 sessions) for context—still an interpretation, not a signal service.

shortBias.simpleHorizon: Last few weeks of price - interpretation, not prediction.
shortBias.notForecast: This is an interpretation of recent price behavior, not a forecast.
shortBias.cryptoFragile: Crypto can move faster and wider than stocks - treat any lean as fragile.
shortBias.confidenceExplain: Confidence reflects internal signal agreement, not probability of outcome.

deeper.behindReadSubtitle: Short notes on what each input is doing - still interpretation, not a data dump.

riskProfile.title: Risk profile
riskProfile.subtitleStocks: How this name tends to behave under stress - qualitative, not a VaR number.
riskProfile.subtitleCrypto: How this asset tends to behave - qualitative, not a model of worst case.
riskProfile.contextStocks: Company news, earnings, and macro can gap price overnight. This page only sees history on the chart.
riskProfile.contextCrypto: Liquidity and sentiment can gap price without a fundamental trigger. Size for venue and tail risk first.

speculative.title: Speculative asset warning
speculative.memeP1: This segment trades on attention and flow, not on cash-flow fundamentals. Narrative shifts can reprice the tape faster than on-chain or technical inputs update.
speculative.memeP2: Outcomes are highly uncertain; drawdowns can arrive quickly when liquidity thins or social bid evaporates.
speculative.otherP1: This symbol sits outside our large-cap basket. Price is more sensitive to depth, wallet flow, and headline risk than to slow fundamental ratios.
speculative.otherP2: Assume wide slippage and gap risk until you independently verify liquidity, venue, and custody.

trade.title: Risk / reward
trade.subtitle: Fixed share count. Dollar risk and reward follow from your prices only - no sizing advice.
trade.perShare: Per share: ${{risk}} at risk, ${{reward}} if target is reached.
trade.shapeLongShort: Long: target above entry, stop below. Short: target below entry, stop above.
trade.shapeBracket: Stop and target must bracket entry for the side you intend.
trade.enterNumeric: Enter numeric prices and a positive share count.

loading.subtitle: Generating a demo interpretation from recent price behavior.

snapshot.title: Structured Interpretation Snapshot
snapshot.subtitle: Clear signal quality in one view - built for first-time users.

simple.upHighVol: Price is mostly moving up, but it can swing hard in both directions.
simple.up: Price is mostly moving up right now.
simple.downHighVol: Price is mostly moving down, and moves can be sharp.
simple.down: Price is mostly moving down right now.
simple.sideways: Price is moving sideways, so direction is not clear yet.

advanced.upStrong: This asset is in an upward trend with strong momentum, showing active buyer control.
advanced.up: This asset is trending upward, but momentum is no longer accelerating.
advanced.downStrong: This asset is in a downward trend with persistent selling pressure.
advanced.down: This asset is trending downward, though downside momentum is less forceful than before.
advanced.sideways: This asset is in a sideways regime, with no durable directional edge in the current window.

simple.highVol: Use smaller size and wait for cleaner moves before taking big risk.
simple.sideways: Waiting for a clearer break can help avoid random chop.
simple.default: The trend may continue, but avoid chasing fast candles.

advanced.highVol: Continuation is possible, but timing quality matters more when volatility expands.
advanced.sideways: With weak directional structure, confirmation is usually more valuable than anticipation.
advanced.default: The current bias supports continuation scenarios, but entries should still respect pullback and risk placement.

tips.trend: Direction over time. Use it to see who has control lately - not to time the next tick.
tips.momentum: Shows how fast price is moving. Slowing often means the trend is losing force.
tips.structure: How orderly moves are. Messy structure means more stop-outs and surprises.
tips.volatility: How far price tends to swing. Higher readings mean larger gap and whip risk.
tips.bias: A one-line lean from several inputs together. It summarizes the setup - not a forecast.
tips.confidence: How much internal signals agree. It is not the odds of being right.
tips.risk: How wild the tape has been lately. High risk means smaller size and more room for error.
tips.marketState: Plain-language snapshot of trend plus pace. Read this before the numbers.
tips.rsi: Tracks whether buying or selling has been persistent. Use with price action, not alone.
tips.compositeLean: A soft tilt from trend, pace, and averages - not a promise of what happens next.

summary.up: Composite skew constructive on this horizon - context for sizing and risk, not a price target.
summary.down: Composite skew defensive on this horizon - one lens; flow and headlines still sit outside this read.
summary.sideways: Composite skew flat - no clean lean from the bundled inputs.

trendMeanings.bullish: Averages and slope suggest buyers are controlling the current window.
trendMeanings.bearish: Price remains under pressure versus slower trend lines in this sample.
trendMeanings.neutral: No durable trend edge: structure is mixed or flat versus moving averages.

momentumMeanings.strong: Recent return and slope align; the move still carries conviction on this horizon.
momentumMeanings.slowing: Direction may still hold, but pace is cooling - often a late-stage signal in swings.
momentumMeanings.weak: Little net progress; impulse is missing even if noise prints both ways.

structureMeanings.stable: Day-to-day swings are contained versus a high-vol regime.
structureMeanings.volatile: Realized variance is elevated; ranges matter more than single levels.

biasMeanings.slightly_bullish: Composite read leans positive without making a forecast claim.
biasMeanings.bearish: Composite read leans negative for this horizon only.
biasMeanings.sideways: Signals conflict or cancel out; treat direction as unresolved.

reasons.trendTooFew: Too few closes to trust SMA20/50 here - trend read is provisional until the window fills.
reasons.trendAboveBothGolden: Price holds above SMA20/50 with the faster line on top - buyers currently control this window. Extension risk rises if the stack gets too vertical.
reasons.trendBelowBothDeath: Price and SMA20 both sit below SMA50 - supply leads this window. Until reclaim, rallies are mostly tactical.
reasons.trendBetween20and50: Price is above SMA20 but below SMA50 - a repair bid, not a clean trend. The next leg depends on whether overhead breaks with participation.
reasons.trendBetween50and20: Price lost SMA20 but still sits above SMA50 - classic pullback versus early roll. Watch whether the slow line survives the next sell wave.
reasons.trendTightBundleSupport: Averages support price in a tight bundle - no winner yet. Coiled setups can run once one side commits.
reasons.trendTightBundlePressure: Averages pressure price in a tight bundle - no winner yet. Coiled setups can run once one side commits.
reasons.momentumBody: ~{{ret}}% over ~20 sessions with a {{slopeWord}} short slope. This tells you whether the tape is paying for trend, not how far it should go.
reasons.rsiHigh: RSI ~{{rsi}}: buying has stayed persistent. Pullback risk increases, but this is not a timing signal for a top.
reasons.rsiLow: RSI ~{{rsi}}: selling has stayed persistent. Exhaustion risk rises, but confirmation from price is still required.
reasons.rsiMid: RSI ~{{rsi}} is mid-band - edge, if any, comes more from slope and averages than from RSI alone.
reasons.volHigh: Realized vol ~{{vol}}% annualized ({{label}}) - ranges and gaps matter more than single prints.
reasons.volLow: Realized vol ~{{vol}}% annualized ({{label}}) - tape is quieter; levels tend to hold until a catalyst appears.

riskExplain.highCrypto: Price is swinging hard - smaller size and wider stops matter more than usual.
riskExplain.highStock: Price swings are large right now - surprises can arrive faster than on a quiet tape.
riskExplain.medium: Moves are normal-to-elevated - plan for two-sided sessions until things calm.
riskExplain.low: Day-to-day moves look relatively contained in this window.

whatToDo.dontRushReversal: Do not rush a reversal - selling pressure is easing, not fully over.
whatToDo.sizeDown: Trend idea may still hold - size down until ranges tighten.
whatToDo.standAside: Stand aside or keep risk small until the next clear move.
whatToDo.upWatchPullback: Trend intact, but watch for pullback risk if pace stays hot.
whatToDo.downPressure: Pressure remains dominant - treat bounces as fragile until proven otherwise.

why.sideways: Recent net change is about {{ret}}%, and the signals still do not align on direction.
why.slowing: Price moved about {{ret}}% in this window, but the speed of the move is cooling.
why.strong: Price moved about {{ret}}% with follow-through still visible in this sample.
why.neutralTrend: Moving averages and slope do not line up into one clean story ({{ret}}% net).
why.default: RSI is around {{rsi}} in this window - use it as context, not as a timer.

marketState.mixed: Mixed - the picture is not clean.

whatToDo.waitBreak: Do not guess - wait for a cleaner break.
whatToDo.dontBuySpike: Do not chase a spike; let it prove itself first.
whatToDo.sizeDown: Use smaller size until things calm down.
whatToDo.default: Only add risk if your plan already defines where you are wrong.

risk.high: Risk is high - large swings are likely.
risk.medium: Risk is medium - normal chop is expected.
risk.low: Risk looks lower here - still not risk-free.

beginner.cryptoWhyAddon: Crypto can print wider swings than large-cap stocks under the same read.
beginner.cryptoSimpleWhyAddon: Crypto usually moves faster than typical stocks.

trend.bullish: Price generally sits above its recent average path in this sample - buyers held the edge.
trend.bearish: Price generally sits below its recent average path - sellers held the edge.
trend.neutral: Up and down forces are balanced in this window - no clean one-way path.

momentum.strong: Moves still carry energy - follow-through remains visible in the data.
momentum.slowing: The move is losing steam - trend can continue, but pay-up risk increases.
momentum.weak: Little net push either way - forcing direction can lead to chop.

structure.stable: Day-to-day swings are milder - levels often matter more than wild gaps.
structure.volatile: Swings are wider - one headline can reprice faster than in a calm tape.

volatility.low: Annualized swing rate looks calm ({{label}}) - not safe, just quieter.
volatility.medium: Annualized swing rate looks moderate ({{label}}) - plan for normal two-way action.
volatility.high: Annualized swing rate looks elevated ({{label}}) - expect larger day-to-day surprises.

rsi.high: Buying has stayed persistent - snapbacks can arrive without warning.
rsi.low: Selling has stayed persistent - relief bounces can appear, but still need proof.
rsi.mid: RSI sits mid-range - edge, if any, comes from trend and pace, not RSI alone.

cryptoNotes.faster: Crypto often moves faster and less predictably than equities.
cryptoNotes.highVol: High volatility environment - avoid oversized positions.
cryptoNotes.wideRange: Ranges are wide - entries and exits need extra margin.
cryptoNotes.memeRisk: Meme/thin-liquidity risk: sentiment can gap price with little warning.

highVol.label: High-volatility expansion
highVol.detail: Realized variance is elevated; day ranges dominate over static levels. Edges decay unless size matches tape conditions.

rangeStable.label: Range / consolidation
rangeStable.detail: Price is compressing with contained disorder - supply and demand are balanced until a catalyst forces repricing.

twoWay.label: Two-way / indecisive
twoWay.detail: Chop without a volatility blow-off: wide ranges, no held break. Two-sided risk dominates until one side commits on closes.

trendContinuation.label: Trend continuation
trendContinuation.detail: Slow structure and short-window impulse align in one direction. The key question is extension versus exhaustion.

transition.label: Transition phase
transition.detail: Time frames disagree or pace is shifting - directional clarity is fading. Assume two-sided risk until one axis wins.

mixed.label: Mixed regime
mixed.detail: Signals do not align into one clean story. Treat tape as unresolved until slope, range, or volatility clearly picks a side.

structuredGeneric.implication: Practical implication: with realized volatility near {{volatility}}% annualized, size and risk control should come first.
structuredGeneric.watchNext: What to watch next: slope inflection, volatility regime shifts, and decisive closes through key swing levels.

biasContextGeneric.sideways: Current bias is neutral and direction remains unconfirmed.
biasContextGeneric.upStrong: Bias leans up with supportive momentum, but extension risk remains.
biasContextGeneric.downStrong: Bias leans down with strong pressure; bounces remain tactical.
biasContextGeneric.slowing: Direction may still hold, but pace is cooling and conviction should be reduced.
biasContextGeneric.default: Treat this read as decision context, not an outcome guarantee.

narrative.watchDefault: What to watch next: slope inflection, realized-vol step-up, or a decisive close through the latest swing high/low.

bearish_trend_up_bias.trend: Trend: averages still lean lower; structure has not flipped.
bearish_trend_up_bias.momentum: Momentum: short-window lift - relief, covering, or flow, not a new backbone yet.
bearish_trend_up_bias.condition: Market condition: bear-market rally / counter-trend noise until reclaim proves.
bearish_trend_up_bias.implication: Practical implication: treat strength as tactical; invalidation comes from failed holds above declining means.
bearish_trend_up_bias.watchNext: What to watch next: whether the lift can close and hold above SMA20 or the prior breakdown pivot.

bullish_trend_sideways_bias.trend: Trend: slow lines remain constructive while fast inputs flatten.
bullish_trend_sideways_bias.momentum: Momentum: digestion phase - pace cooled without breaking larger skew.
bullish_trend_sideways_bias.condition: Market condition: pause/flag inside a broader higher drift.
bullish_trend_sideways_bias.implication: Practical implication: directional conviction is medium; stale longs face time decay without a fresh catalyst.
bullish_trend_sideways_bias.watchNext: What to watch next: renewed push with participation, or a break of the latest higher low.

up_slow_bear_trend.trend: Trend: negative backbone; bounces lose increment.
up_slow_bear_trend.momentum: Momentum: lift fading into overhead - slope flattening, not accelerating.
up_slow_bear_trend.condition: Market condition: rallys sold into supply until a higher low forms on volume.
up_slow_bear_trend.implication: Practical implication: longs need a reclaim thesis; shorts still control time until structure improves.
up_slow_bear_trend.watchNext: What to watch next: failed reclaim at SMA20 / latest lower high, plus volatility expansion on down sessions.

up_slow.trend: Trend: composite still constructive, but edge is thinning.
up_slow.momentum: Momentum: slowing - return on directional risk is fading day by day.
up_slow.condition: Market condition: mean-reversion and drift risk rise before trend is fully broken.
up_slow.implication: Practical implication: size for chop; fresh directional adds carry only moderate-to-low conviction.
up_slow.watchNext: What to watch next: whether the next dip holds the last swing low with narrowing volatility.

up_strong_vol.trend: Trend: bid is real - price advanced with positive short slope.
up_strong_vol.momentum: Momentum: strong (~{{ret}}% over ~20 sessions) but noisy.
up_strong_vol.condition: Market condition: high realized volatility (~{{vol}}% annualized) - ranges dominate static levels.
up_strong_vol.implication: Practical implication: risk budget should be volatility-first; direction alone is incomplete without gap control.
up_strong_vol.watchNext: What to watch next: volatility compression vs expansion, and whether closes cluster near highs or revert mid-range.

up_strong.trend: Trend: aligned upward - slow structure and impulse agree in this window.
up_strong.momentum: Momentum: strong - ~{{ret}}% net with slope still positive.
up_strong.condition: Market condition: continuation profile until extension invites mean reversion.
up_strong.implication: Practical implication: define wrong by time and level - tighten risk if price stretches too far from SMA20.
up_strong.watchNext: What to watch next: first close below a rising SMA20, or volatility spike without new highs.

up_weak.trend: Trend: composite reads up, but tape does not fully cooperate.
up_weak.momentum: Momentum: weak - follow-through is missing despite positive bias.
up_weak.condition: Market condition: episodic flow or passive bid, not broad re-risking.
up_weak.implication: Practical implication: avoid chasing pop moves until slope re-accelerates with broader participation.
up_weak.watchNext: What to watch next: volume balance on up vs down sessions, and slope re-acceleration vs failure.

down_strong_vol.trend: Trend: distribution has both pace and width.
down_strong_vol.momentum: Momentum: strong downside - slope ~{{slope}}%/day with elevated volatility.
down_strong_vol.condition: Market condition: expansion (~{{vol}}% annualized) - counter-trend bounces are rented, not owned.
down_strong_vol.implication: Practical implication: wait for supply to clear or range to compress before sizing reversal risk.
down_strong_vol.watchNext: What to watch next: range lower band behavior, capitulation volume, or orderly drift lower.

down_strong.trend: Trend: sellers control closes; slope stays negative with sponsorship.
down_strong.momentum: Momentum: strong - downside closes cluster; bounces remain tactical.
down_strong.condition: Market condition: continuation lower until time or price stabilizes.
down_strong.implication: Practical implication: oversold can stay oversold; timing turns needs flow evidence this stack does not include.
down_strong.watchNext: What to watch next: volume dry-up on declines, or failed new lows after high print.

down_weak.momentum: Momentum: weak - grind, not stampede; impulse is thin on both sides.
down_weak.condition: Market condition: apathy risk - tape can extend on absent bid as much as on fear.
down_weak.implication: Practical implication: fades need a catalyst; oscillator alone is insufficient.
down_weak.watchNext: What to watch next: macro/idiosyncratic headlines, or a volatility lift that breaks the grind.

sideways_volatile.condition: Market condition: range-like but wide - two-way inventory, no held break.
sideways_volatile.implication: Practical implication: directional conviction is low; false breaks are more common than clean narratives.
sideways_volatile.watchNext: What to watch next: close outside the latest 10-20 day box with follow-through next session.

sideways_stable_midrsi.trend: Trend: neutral - no skew from slow lines in this sample.
sideways_stable_midrsi.momentum: Momentum: balanced - RSI mid ({{rsi}}), returns non-trending.
sideways_stable_midrsi.condition: Market condition: equilibrium - matched supply/demand in tighter volatility.
sideways_stable_midrsi.implication: Practical implication: paying for direction is expensive; breakout attempts require clean follow-through.

sideways_stable.trend: Trend: flat composite with no clear initiative owner.
sideways_stable.condition: Market condition: low disorder, indecisive - range or drift inside a band.
sideways_stable.implication: Practical implication: keep risk flat or edge-only until a catalyst forces one side.
sideways_stable.watchNext: What to watch next: volatility lift, gap, or flow shift that breaks local equilibrium.

sideways_rsi_hi.trend: Trend: sideways composite despite stretched oscillator.
sideways_rsi_hi.momentum: Momentum: RSI elevated ({{rsi}}) - upside already paid for in this window.
sideways_rsi_hi.condition: Market condition: chop near upper band - sentiment slips can gap quickly.
sideways_rsi_hi.implication: Practical implication: long gamma gets crowded; keep engagement tight if any.
sideways_rsi_hi.watchNext: What to watch next: failed highs with rising volatility, or absorption at highs under thin supply.

sideways_rsi_lo.trend: Trend: sideways; oscillator washed but structure has not flipped.
sideways_rsi_lo.momentum: Momentum: RSI low ({{rsi}}) - short-term selling may be exhausted.
sideways_rsi_lo.condition: Market condition: bounce-prone into overhead until trend proves.
sideways_rsi_lo.implication: Practical implication: bounces are tactical into supply, not structural flips, without reclaim.
sideways_rsi_lo.watchNext: What to watch next: reclaim of SMA20 / prior breakdown, plus volume on first up sessions.

default.trendTilt: Trend: {{trend}} tilt, not unanimous across windows.
default.condition: Market condition: {{structure}} tape with conflicting fast/slow reads.
default.implication: Practical implication: keep conviction low; wait for slope, range, or volatility to break cleanly first.

momWord.strong: Impulse still pays for directional risk.
momWord.slowing: Follow-through is cooling after the latest leg.
momWord.weak: Impulse is thin - moves lack sponsorship.

biasContext.bearish_trend_up_bias: Relief lift into a still-negative average stack - strength stays tactical until reclaim proves.
biasContext.bullish_trend_sideways_bias: Slow trend remains intact while fast inputs stall - digestion, not a confirmed handoff lower.
biasContext.up_slow_bear_trend: Bounces fade against a weak backbone - sellers still control time without a higher low.
biasContext.up_slow: Composite still leans up, but pace is cooling - mean-reversion risk rises before trend breaks.
biasContext.up_strong_vol: Upside impulse competes with wide day ranges - edge is as much volatility as direction.
biasContext.up_strong: Trend and impulse align constructively - risk is extension, not absence of bid.
biasContext.up_weak: Positive bias without follow-through - flow looks episodic, not sustained.
biasContext.down_strong_vol: Selling has velocity and width - counter-trend needs time or cleared supply.
biasContext.down_strong: Downside has sponsorship in closes - bounces stay tactical until structure flips.
biasContext.down_weak: Soft lower drift - low urgency, but no mandate to fade without catalyst.
biasContext.sideways_volatile: Two-way tape: momentum and trend do not provide a clean skew inside wide ranges.
biasContext.sideways_stable_midrsi: Equilibrium: oscillator mid-band, volatility contained - market is not forcing a side.
biasContext.sideways_stable: Flat bias with quieter ranges - wait for break rather than forcing a narrative.
biasContext.sideways_rsi_hi: Flat bias with stretched RSI - upside can be crowded and vulnerable to air pockets.
biasContext.sideways_rsi_lo: Flat bias with washed RSI - short covering is possible, but overhead supply still matters.
biasContext.default: Time frames disagree enough that the next leg belongs to whichever axis breaks first.

decision.notAdvice: Educational interpretation only. Not financial advice. Apply your own plan and risk rules.

why.title: Why this read?
why.subtitle: Three inputs we weight before suggesting an action.

combination.risky: Conditions are stressed—prioritize capital preservation and avoid adding risk until the tape stabilizes.
combination.exitDefensive: Pressure and risk are elevated—defensive positioning is appropriate until structure improves.
combination.volatileSideways: Wide ranges without a clean trend—false moves are common; wait for a decisive close outside the range.
combination.volatileTrend: A directional lean exists, but volatility is high—size down and plan for two-way noise.
combination.neutralWait: No durable edge yet—patience usually beats forcing a direction in this regime.
combination.wait: Signals disagree or risk is unclear—standing aside preserves optionality.
combination.enterSmall: There is opportunity, but uncertainty is meaningful—only small, well-defined risk is appropriate.
combination.hold: Conditions are relatively orderly and the lean is constructive—maintain discipline without adding aggressive risk.
combination.trendingCalm: Trend and volatility are aligned in a calmer band—continuation is plausible, but still manage extension risk.
combination.downtrend: Selling pressure dominates this window—favor defense, smaller size, and clear invalidation until structure improves.
combination.default: Use this read as context, then align entries, size, and invalidation with your own plan.

wait.1: The setup is not clean enough to justify a confident directional step.
wait.2: When signals conflict or the tape is choppy, waiting for confirmation usually reduces costly mistakes.
wait.3: Confidence is {{confidence}}%—treat this as context, not certainty.

waitVolatile.1: Volatility is elevated, so outcomes can swing quickly even if a bias exists.
waitVolatile.2: Smaller size and clearer levels matter more until the tape compresses or proves a break.

enterSmall.1: There is a constructive lean for {{asset}}, but risk is not negligible.
enterSmall.2: If you participate, keep risk small and define where the idea is wrong before you add.

hold.1: {{asset}} shows a relatively orderly constructive lean in this window.
hold.2: If you are already aligned with your plan, the priority is discipline—not chasing marginal extension.

exit.1: Downside pressure or risk is dominant in this read—defensive action fits the current tape.
exit.2: This is not a timing guarantee; it is a risk-first interpretation of recent behavior.

up.1: Price behavior recently skews upward, but pace and volatility still matter for entries.
up.2: Confidence is {{confidence}}%—higher agreement is not the same as a guaranteed outcome.

down.1: Price behavior recently skews downward; respect the trend until evidence of stabilization.
down.2: Confidence is {{confidence}}%—use it as a quality gauge for how aligned the inputs are.

neutral.1: The tape is mixed—direction is not the main story yet.
neutral.2: Confidence is {{confidence}}%—when agreement is lower, smaller risk and clearer triggers help.

advanced.title: Charts & detailed analytics
advanced.subtitle: Open when you want the full stack—same engine, more granularity.

behavior.WAIT: Wait for confirmation or cleaner momentum before committing size.
behavior.ENTER_SMALL: If you act, use a small slice of capital—risk is meaningful and surprises are more likely.
behavior.HOLD: If your plan already matches this lean, keep size steady and avoid adding heat without new evidence.
behavior.EXIT: Reduce exposure or step aside until risk and structure look healthier.

positionSize.waitExit: Use 0% of new capital—preserve optionality until the read stabilizes.
positionSize.hold: Consider keeping existing risk flat; optional adds only if your rules already allow them.
positionSize.enterSmallHigh: If engaging, think 5% of capital or less, with a hard stop and a defined invalidation.
positionSize.enterSmallMod: If engaging, think 5–10% of capital with strict risk limits.
positionSize.enterSmallLow: If engaging, up to ~10% of capital may still be aggressive—scale to your personal risk tolerance.

invalidation.WAIT: If momentum strengthens and price closes decisively through the recent range, re-evaluate.
invalidation.ENTER_SMALL: If volatility spikes further or your stop level is touched, pause and reassess.
invalidation.HOLD: If the trend weakens materially or risk jumps a tier, re-evaluate size and stops.
invalidation.EXIT: If momentum eases and price stabilizes above key short-term support, you can revisit with a fresh read.

positionCalc.title: Position size calculator
positionCalc.subtitle: Estimate size from capital at risk—not a recommendation to trade.
positionCalc.explainUnits: Units = dollars at risk ÷ per-unit risk (distance from entry to stop).
positionCalc.explainLoss: Max loss is what you would lose if the stop is hit (before fees/slippage).
positionCalc.explainProfit: Potential profit assumes the take-profit level is reached.
positionCalc.invalid: Enter positive capital, risk % between 0.1 and 100, valid prices, and ensure stop is on the correct side of entry.
positionCalc.disclaimer: Educational tool only. Not financial advice.

aiAssistant.title: Stock Sense AI
aiAssistant.subtitle: Educational market assistant
aiAssistant.intro: Ask how to read trend, momentum, volatility, or risk. This assistant does not predict prices or tell you what to buy.
aiAssistant.q1: How should I read confidence on this app?
aiAssistant.q2: What does higher volatility imply for position size?
aiAssistant.q3: How is momentum different from trend?
aiAssistant.disclaimer: Educational only. Not financial advice. No trade signals.
aiAssistant.blocked: I can’t recommend specific assets. But I can help you understand how to evaluate them using trend, momentum, and volatility. Ask how to read those signals or what they mean for risk—not which name to pick.
aiAssistant.welcome: Ask a question below, or tap a suggested prompt.
